title: DNA extraction from gram negative bacteria
author: Louise Judd
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.p5mdq46

steps.title: Inoculate single bacterial colony into 2...
steps.instruction: Inoculate single bacterial colony into 2 ml of appropriate growth media

steps.title: Incubate overnight 37 o C with shaking
steps.instruction: Incubate overnight 37 o C with shaking

steps.title: Transfer 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 1.5 ml of overnight culture into microfuge tube

steps.title: Remove supernatant NOTE At this stage...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant NOTE At this stage bacterial pellet can be sored at -80 o C for gDNA extraction at a later date with no compromise to extraction

steps.title: Dissolve lypholised RNase A American Bioanalytical #AB
steps.instruction: Dissolve lypholised RNase A American Bioanalytical #AB

steps.title: in 50% glycerol and 10 mM Tris (pH 8
steps.instruction: in 50% glycerol and 10 mM Tris (pH 8.

steps.title: to a final concentration of 100...
steps.instruction: to a final concentration of 100 mg/ml (stock can be sorted at -20 o C for future use)

steps.title: Dissolve lypholised Genfind proteinase K in...
steps.instruction: Dissolve lypholised Genfind proteinase K in Genfind proteinase K buffer to a final concentration of 96 mg/ml (stock can be sorted at -20 o C for future use)

steps.title: For each bacterial ioslate to be...
steps.instruction: For each bacterial ioslate to be extracted make a lysis master mix containing 400 ul Genfind lysis buffer, 9 ul Genfind proteinsae K and 1 ul RNase A (see

steps.title: Add 400 ul of the lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 400 ul of the lysis master mix to each bacterial pellet

steps.title: Gently tip mix 10 times to...
steps.instruction: Gently tip mix 10 times to enusre the bacterial pellet is well resuspended

steps.title: Incubate at 37 o C for 30 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 o C for 30 minutes

steps.title: Procedd to DNA extraction
steps.instruction: Procedd to DNA extraction

steps.title: Invert the Genfind binding buffer 20...
steps.instruction: Invert the Genfind binding buffer 20 times to ensure complete resuspension of the magnetic particles

steps.title: Add 300 ul of the Genfind...
steps.instruction: Add 300 ul of the Genfind binding buffer to each of the samples and gently tip mix 10 times, avoid generating bubbles. The magentic particles will most likely clump together at this stage due to the high concentration of DNA in the sample.

steps.title: Incubate samples at room temperature for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate samples at room temperature for 5 minutes

steps.title: Place the sample on a magentic...
steps.instruction: Place the sample on a magentic rack for 5 minutes or until solution completely clears

steps.title: With the tubes on the magnet...
steps.instruction: With the tubes on the magnet remove the supernatant avoiding the magentic particles

steps.title: Remove samples from the magnet
steps.instruction: Remove samples from the magnet

steps.title: Add 800 ul of Genfind wash...
steps.instruction: Add 800 ul of Genfind wash buffer 1 to each of the samples

steps.title: Gently tip mix each sample 10 times
steps.instruction: Gently tip mix each sample 10 times. The idea is to try and resuspend the magentic beads but this will most likely not be possible as they will clump together. I try to make sure the clumps of beads move in and out of the pipette tip with each mix

steps.title: Place the sample on a magentic...
steps.instruction: Place the sample on a magentic rack for 5 minutes or until solution completely clears

steps.title: Repeat steps 7-10 for a total...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 7-10 for a total of two washes with Genfind wash buffer 1

steps.title: Add 500 ul of Genfind wash...
steps.instruction: Add 500 ul of Genfind wash buffer 2 to each of the samples

steps.title: Gently tip mix each sample 10 times
steps.instruction: Gently tip mix each sample 10 times. The idea is to try and resuspend the magentic beads but this will most likely not be possible as they will clump together. I try to make sure the clumps of beads move in and out of the pipette tip with each mix

steps.title: Place the sample on a magentic...
steps.instruction: Place the sample on a magentic rack for 5 minutes or until solution completely clears

steps.title: Repeat steps 11-13 for a total...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 11-13 for a total of two washes with Genfind wash buffer 2

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge samples to bring all...
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge samples to bring all liquid to the bottom of the tube

steps.title: Place the sample on a magentic...
steps.instruction: Place the sample on a magentic rack for 5 minutes or until solution completely clears

steps.title: Remove all traces of wash buffer...
steps.instruction: Remove all traces of wash buffer 2 with a pipette, but do not allow beads to fully dry out.

steps.title: Add 50-200 ul of 10 mM Tris pH 8
steps.instruction: Add 50-200 ul of 10 mM Tris pH 8.0 to each sample (I usually start with 100 ul but it will depend on the groth dynamics of your baccteria)

steps.title: Remove samples from the magnet
steps.instruction: Remove samples from the magnet

steps.title: Gently tip mix each sample 10...
steps.instruction: Gently tip mix each sample 10 times and incubate at 37 o C for 5 minutes then tip mix again. To ensure elution of DNA off the particles it is crucial that the magentic particles are completely resuspended. If it is not possble to entirle resuspend particles then add more 10 mM Tris.

steps.title: Place the sample on a magentic...
steps.instruction: Place the sample on a magentic rack for 5 minutes or until solution completely clears

steps.title: Tranfer supenatant to a clean microfuge tube
steps.instruction: Tranfer supenatant to a clean microfuge tube. Try to avoid carryover of magnetic particles into the supernatant. NOTE After extraction DNA should be stored at 4 o C to minimise any damage. Storage at -20 o C will result in ice crystal formation that will shear you DNA. I try to do my DNA extractions on the smae day as my library prep but have stored DNA at 4 o C for a couple of weeks and still amde sucessful libraries.

steps.title: 1) Visualise sample to estimate the molecular weight
steps.instruction: 1) Visualise sample to estimate the molecular weight. We have a Fragment Analyzer and run a gDNA 1-60knp gel. Once you have extracted gDNA from the same species a number of times it is possibly not necessary to run a "gel" wiht each subsequent extraction. However, I would run QC gels each time you tackle a new bacterial speices 2) Quantify your sample with a fluorimter er. QUANTUS of Qubit. 3)Analyse 1 µL in a UV spectrophotometer (e.g. Nanodrop).
